ai_insights.executive_summary: NYOH's February 2026 performance reflects a network average composite score of 50.0, with our seven clinics ranging from 31 to 51 out of 100. The network achieved scheduler compliance of 69.66% and an average patient delay of 7.28 minutes, though performance is distributed unevenly across sites. Month-over-month, we saw meaningful improvements in overtime management and scheduling discipline at three clinics, alongside emerging strengths in chair utilization at AMC and AMS.
highlights: HUD demonstrated a 27.9 percentage point improvement in scheduler compliance, rising from 42.6% in January to 54.5% in February, alongside a 50% reduction in treatments running past closing from 0.5 to 0.3 daily. AMC's chair utilization grew 23.7 percentage points from 65.7% in January to 81.2% in February, now operating at 1.6 standard deviations above the clinic group mean of 53.7%, while overtime treatments per day declined 22.1% from 0.9 to 0.7 over the same period.
concerns: AMC's average patient delay of 15.6 minutes is more than double the company average of 7.28 minutes—a 114% gap—and represents the network's largest single performance opportunity despite strong chair asset utilization. We noticed HUD carries nursing resource constraints, with nurse utilization at 39.6% (2.0 standard deviations below the clinic group mean of 49.7%), which may be limiting patient throughput relative to available capacity, and we'd welcome exploring whether staffing or skill-mix adjustments could help unlock utilization closer to the 49.7% group norm.
recommendations: We recommend AMC conduct a structured audit of scheduling templates and nurse-to-chair assignment logic within iOptimize to understand why high chair utilization (81.2%) coexists with elevated delays (15.6 mins vs. 7.28 mins network average). Specifically, reviewing whether late-afternoon appointment clustering or nurse bottlenecks during peak hours may be compressing patient flow would help align scheduling discipline with the clinic's strong asset efficiency.

ai_insights.executive_summary: NYOH delivered mixed March 2026 performance with a network average Performance Score of 39.7 out of 100 (where 50 = network average), slightly below the company benchmark. Six of seven clinics showed month-over-month improvements in at least one operational metric, with AMS rising to 55/100 and AMC improving treatments past closing from 0.7 to 0.5 daily. However, four clinics remain 14+ points below the 50-point benchmark, and scheduler compliance across NYOH averages 70.5% against a company average of 73.96%, suggesting systematic scheduling discipline gaps.
highlights: AMS demonstrated the strongest operational performance in March, achieving a Performance Score of 55 out of 100 (where 50 = network average) and chair utilization of 84.8%, a 28.1-point increase from 66.2% in February, while maintaining average patient delays of just 1.6 minutes. TROY improved chair utilization by 37.4% from 30.9% in February to 42.5% in March, alongside a multi-month upward trend in scheduler compliance now averaging 78.1% across the latest period.
concerns: We noticed HUD carries two operational red flags: iAssign utilization sitting at 95.5%, 1.6 standard deviations below the group mean of 98.9%, and nurse utilization at 40.5%, 1.9 standard deviations below the group mean of 53.0% and 4-month average of 39.2%. Separately, A210 presents the largest performance gap in our network: average patient delay of 15.9 minutes against a company average of 7.7 minutes (a 106% gap), despite showing month-over-month improvement in minutes past closing from 16.4 to 10.5 in March.
recommendations: We recommend auditing A210's chair-to-nurse assignment workflow and reviewing its scheduling templates against iOptimize recommendations, given the 106% delay gap versus the company average of 7.7 minutes. Scheduler compliance at A210 averages 69.9% over three months, below the company average of 73.96%, and we suspect misalignment between recommended appointment slots and actual clinical capacity is coinciding with the extended waits. A structured review of how iAssign assignments map to nurse availability and chair scheduling would help identify friction points.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
